title: drosera stricticaulis subsp. eremaea
intro: Drosera stricticaulis subsp. eremaea, also known as the Western Australian sundew, is a rare carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Droseraceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Drosera stricticaulis subsp. eremaea include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to harness its medicinal properties.
